photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: an underground nuclear waste facility suffers a critical breach, triggering a massive regional evacuation
the first human brain-computer interface is successfully implanted, allowing telepathic communication
a new designer drug takes the world by storm, causing a major public health crisis
a military coup overthrows the government of a nuclear-armed nation in a sudden overnight raid
massive volcanic eruption in the middle of a city
a massive synthetic biology lab leak creates a highly aggressive, invasive plant species that destroys concrete

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a local high school wins a national science championship, receiving a hero's welcome in their hometown
a major social media scandal, such as the cambridge analytica scandal or the instagram algorithm controversy
a natural phenomenon, such as a solar eclipse or meteor shower, that captures the world's attention
a major civil rights movement, such as the fight for lgbtq+ equality or women's rights
a new high-speed rail line is inaugurated, connecting major cities and reducing travel times
a major development in international relations, such as a new trade deal or a new peace treaty

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a historic environmental recovery: the bald eagle population is officially declared fully recovered and removed from the endangered species list
a bold environmental protest: activists scale a major oil rig in the north sea, unfurling a massive banner protesting deep-sea drilling
a dramatic urban rescue: firefighters work tirelessly to rescue residents trapped on the upper floors of a burning skyscraper in downtown chicago
a major world crisis: the covid-19 pandemic sweeps across the globe, leading to millions of deaths and upending daily life for billions of people
a technological breakthrough: scientists create a new type of battery that is both more efficient and less expensive than existing technologies, revolutionizing the energy industry
a massive wildfire: a fire sweeps through the australian outback, destroying homes and forcing thousands of residents to evacuate

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: candid photography: capturing the spontaneous moments of protests and the raw emotions of the participants
extreme wide-angle photography: using wide lenses to include both the protesters in the foreground and the grand architectural context in the background
cinematic photojournalism: utilizing anamorphic lenses, wide aspect ratios, and dramatic color grading to evoke a movie-like atmosphere
high-contrast photography: emphasizing the stark contrasts between light and dark to create a bold and impactful image
underwater and rain-spattered photography: shooting through wet lenses or during heavy downpours to capture the grueling physical conditions of outdoor protests
retro photography: using vintage or outdated equipment to create a unique and stylized look

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: aviation and aerospace photojournalism, covering space launches, aviation shows, and aerospace engineering
coverage of political rallies and protests
citizen photojournalism, featuring raw and immediate coverage of breaking news by eyewitnesses
conflict resolution and peacekeeping missions, focusing on diplomatic efforts and peacekeepers in the field

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A person playing a harp strung with rainbows, creating music that colors the air.
An empty warehouse dynamically transformed by flora that explode from the ground.
A dramatic scene of two cars colliding at an intersection, with shattered glass and debris flying in the air, capturing the intensity and impact of the crash.
A white cat driving in a car through a busy downtown street with tall buildings and pedestrians in the background
A woman sipping a steaming cup of tea.
A coconut tree made of dollar bills at sunset, with bills falling off like leaves.

set.parti.title: PartiPrompts
set.parti.description: PartiPrompts (P2) is a rich set of over 1600 prompts in English. P2 can be used to measure model capabilities across various categories and challenge aspects.
examples: A helicopter flies over the Grand Canyon.
a red train is coming down the beach
an abstract painting of three triangles in blue, yellow and red
Portrait of a gecko wearing a train conductor’s hat and holding a flag that has a yin-yang symbol on it. Marble statue.
